questions.prompt_html: A lamp is shining on top of a vertical light post that is 540 centimetres tall. questions.prompt_text: A lamp is shining on top of a vertical light post that is 540 centimetres tall. A girl is running away from the base of the light post at a constant rate so that her shadow is increasing at the rate of 15 cm/s and the tip of her shadow is moving away from the base at the rate of 90 cm/s. How tall is she?
questions.solution_html:
Let x be length of shadow, y be the distance from the tip of the shadow to the base of the light post and L be the girl's height (as shown in the figure below).

From similarity,  =  ; hence 540 x = L y; L is a constant!
Differentiating both sides with respect to time we obtain 540  = L
Substituting  = rate of change of shadow = +15 cm / s and 
  = rate of change of distance between the tip of the shadow and the base of the light post = + 90 cm /s, we obtain 540(15) = L (90). Therefore 
L =  = 90 cm.
The girl is 90 cm tall (about 3 feet ).
questions.solution_text: Let x be length of shadow, y be the distance from the tip of the shadow to the base of the light post and L be the girl's height (as shown in the figure below). From similarity, (540/y) = (L/x) ; hence 540 x = L y; L is a constant! Differentiating both sides with respect to time we obtain 540 (dx/dt) = L (dy/dt) Substituting (dx/dt) = rate of change of shadow = +15 cm / s and (dy/dt) = rate of change of distance between the tip of the shadow and the base of the light post = + 90 cm /s, we obtain 540(15) = L (90). Therefore L = ((540(15)/90) = 90 cm. The girl is 90 cm tall (about 3 feet ). Short Description: An illustration shows a right triangle. The right triangle has altitude 540 cm and base y. A vertical line from hypotenuse to the base forms another right triangle on the left. The altitude of this right triangle is L and base is x.

choices.label: A
choices.html: L(x) =  -
choices.text: L(x) = (π/6) - (square root of (3) x/2 - square root of (3))

choices.label: B
choices.html: L(x) =  +
choices.text: L(x) = (π/6) + ((2 - square root of (3) )x/square root of (3))

choices.label: C
choices.html: L(x) =  + (3 + 3)x

choices.label: D
choices.html: L(x) =  -
